narrative:
On June 17, 2026, the Karnataka High Court recorded an overall hearing rate of 43.5%, with 1,157 cases heard out of 2,661 scheduled across 47 judges. This activity marks a decline in efficiency from the 47.9% hearing rate (1,321 of 2,760 scheduled) recorded on June 16 and the 48.9% rate on June 15, though it remains higher than the 40.1% clearance rate observed on June 12.

Individual judge performance showed significant variation. Justice S Vishwajith Shetty managed the court's heaviest docket, hearing 92 out of 262 scheduled cases (35.1%). Justice M. Nagaprasanna demonstrated strong output, hearing 58 of 75 scheduled cases (77.3%) in his primary list and 70 of 129 (54.3%) in his second. In contrast, Justice Ashok S. Kinagi converted only 12.8% of his largest batch, hearing 16 of 125 scheduled cases.

An analysis of hearing stages shows that cases listed for "Orders" represented the largest share of the day's schedule, with 462 of 946 cases heard (48.8%). This is a decrease from the 53.6% conversion rate for Orders on June 16. The "Admission" stage converted at 43.9% (191 of 435), while "Final Hearing" stages yielded the lowest conversion, with only 24.7% (122 of 494) of listed cases proceeding to a hearing.

Among case types, Writ Petitions (WP) heavily dominated the docket with 1,235 scheduled cases, of which 462 were heard (37.4%). This represents a drop from the 44.7% hearing rate for WPs on June 16. Criminal Petitions (CRL.P) recorded a higher hearing rate of 54.0% (189 of 350), while Miscellaneous First Appeals (MFA) concluded with 149 of 345 cases heard (43.2%).
